gist.headline: Blame the gateway, not the user
gist.blurb: We fixed a sneaky bug where Azure's internal blocks were returning a 403 error, making it look like you did something wrong. It now correctly returns a 502 Bad Gateway so you know it's an upstream hiccup.
gist.summary: Remaps Azure's 403 errors for gptimage to 502 Bad Gateway so clients correctly identify upstream blocks rather than client-side authentication issues.
gist.impact: Developers will now receive a 502 status code instead of a 403 when Azure blocks an image generation request, making debugging much clearer.
gist.visual_concept: The Nomnom creature eating a bad '403' error log while the bee mascot happily holds up a shiny new '502' sign. Represents fixing the error code routing.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan Nomnom creature happily eating a red '403' error document while the cute bee mascot holds up a shiny new '502' sign. A retro server rack glows softly in the background. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like A Short Hike. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes.
